news.t: Bitcoin's high conviction holders are turning into sellers as the crypto's price hits new lows
Real Assets or Active ETFs? Where RIAs Allocate

overview.description: The AGF U.S. Market Neutral Anti-Beta Fund (BTAL) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in long/short alternatives. The fund is an actively managed fund that takes long positions in low beta US stocks, offset by short positions in high beta US stocks. BTAL was launched on Sep 13, 2011 and is issued by AGF.
infoTable: Dow Jones U.S. Thematic Market Neutral Low Beta Index

data.title: Bitcoin's high conviction holders are turning into sellers as the crypto's price hits new lows
data.text: In the past two days, bitcoin's highest conviction holders have sold about $2.4 billion in bitcoin. Of the bitcoin sold in the past 30 days, 26% came from investors who bought it above $90,000.

data.title: Real Assets or Active ETFs? Where RIAs Allocate
data.text: Registered investment advisors did not pull back on exchange-traded funds in the first quarter of 2026. They kept adding them, according to AdvizorPro's Q1 2026 Quarterly RIA ETF Trends Report.

data.title: Here are some ETFs that protect against inflation risk, with Astoria Portfolio Advisors' John Davi
data.text: John Davi, Astoria Portfolio Advisors Founder & CEO, joins CNBC's Courtney Reagan on ‘ETF Edge' to discuss Astoria's top ideas on how to hedge against tariff risks.
